title: lupinus kingii var. argillaceus
intro: Lupinus kingii var. argillaceus, also known as the Argentine lupine, is a variety of lupine that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a perennial legume that has medicinal properties due to its high concentration of alkaloids, specifically lupinine and sparteine, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, lower blood pressure, and act as a natural diuretic. Lupinus kingii var. argillaceus can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used to treat conditions like arthritis, fever, and urinary tract infections.
